title: Fiery Silky Jamaican Turkey Sushi
description: Savory, silky, indulgent Jamaican-inspired sushi made with plant-based turkey, coconut-soy sauce, and a fiery scotch bonnet kick, served in a copper mug.
instructions: Rinse the jasmine rice until water runs clear, then soak it in water for 20 minutes.
Drain the rice and steam it in a steamer basket over boiling water for 18 minutes, then let it sit covered for 5 minutes.
While rice cooks, press firm tofu to remove excess moisture, then cut it into 1 cm cubes.

In a small saucepan, combine coconut milk, soy sauce, lime juice, minced garlic, grated ginger, allspice, thyme, and a pinch of salt; heat over medium until the mixture just simmers, then reduce heat to low and set aside.
Heat a tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, add the tofu cubes, and stir-fry for 6-7 minutes until golden and crisp on all sides.
Add the finely chopped scotch bonnet pepper to the skillet and cook for another 2 minutes, letting the heat infuse the tofu.
Pour the warmed sauce over the tofu and stir to coat, cooking for an additional 3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Remove the tofu mixture from heat and let it cool for 3 minutes, then gently fold in the steamed rice, pressing lightly to form small rice balls that resemble nigiri.
Transfer the rice balls into a clean copper mug, placing a few pieces of tofu on top of each rice ball.
Sprinkle toasted cashew nuts over the tofu-topped rice for crunch and a few drops of lime juice for brightness.
